introduction:
The Ration Card(Rice card) in Andhra Pradesh is issued by the Department of Consumer Affairs, Food & Civil Supplies, Government of Andhra Pradesh. It entitles eligible families to receive subsidised food grains — rice, wheat, sugar, and kerosene — through the Public Distribution System (PDS) at their designated Fair Price Shop (FPS).

The ration card serves as a crucial identity and address proof accepted across government services. Andhra Pradesh distributes ration cards under the National Food Security Act (NFSA) as well as state-specific schemes like Anna Canteen and NTR Bharosa Pension Scheme.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Applying for a new ration card is FREE of cost at Secretariats (nominal user charge may apply online)
  • Processing time is approximately 21 to 30 working days
  • You must be a permanent resident of Andhra Pradesh to apply
  • Only one ration card per family is permitted
  • Aadhaar linking is mandatory for all family members
  • Track applications at apseva.ap.gov.in or via your local Volunteer
  • Helpline: 1967 (PDS) or 1902 (AP Seva / Sachivalayam)
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Ration Card

Note: Submit self-attested photocopies at the office and carry original documents for verification by the officer.
Identity Proof (Any one)
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Voter ID Card
  • PAN Card
  • Passport
  • Driving Licence
  • MNREGA Job Card
Address Proof (Any one)
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Electricity Bill (recent)
  • Water / Telephone Bill
  • Bank Passbook
  • Registered Rent Agreement
  • Property Tax Receipt
Income & Family Documents
  • Income Certificate (from MRO / Tahsildar)
  • Birth Certificates of children
  • Marriage Certificate (if applicable)
  • Passport-size photos of all family members
Category Guide
  • AAY (Priority — White): Poorest of poor families
  • PHH (Priority Household): NFSA beneficiaries
  • NPHH (Non-Priority): Above poverty line families

Note: BPL/PHH category requires income certificate from MRO.
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via AP Seva Portal

Andhra Pradesh offers an integrated online service application system through the AP Seva Portal. You need an Aadhaar-linked mobile number to proceed.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit the AP Seva Portal
    Go to apseva.ap.gov.in
  2. Citizen Login
    Click on "Login" and select Citizen Login. Enter your Aadhaar number or mobile number, and authenticate using the OTP sent to your registered mobile.
  3. Select Service
    Navigate to the Civil Supplies department section and select "Issue of New Rice Card / Ration Card".
  4. Fill Application Form
    Enter all required details accurately:
    • Head of family details (as per Aadhaar)
    • Addition of all family members with their Aadhaar numbers
    • e-KYC authentication details
    • Selection of your respective Grama/Ward Secretariat and nearest FPS
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload clear scanned copies of identity proof, address proof, income certificate, and family photograph. Ensure files are within the specified size limit (usually 200 KB per file in JPG/PDF format).
  6. Submit & Note Application Number
    Submit your application and pay the nominal service fee if applicable. You will receive an Application Number / Transaction ID via SMS for tracking your status.
  7. Verification by Volunteer / Secretariat Staff
    Your application will be routed to your local Village/Ward Secretariat. A Grama/Ward Volunteer or authorized staff will conduct a physical field verification at your home.
Tip: For a faster process, you can also directly visit your nearest Grama/Ward Sachivalayam (Secretariat) with all your original documents.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at Grama/Ward Sachivalayam (Secretariat)

The most common and accessible way to apply for a new Rice Card is through your local Village or Ward Secretariat (Grama / Ward Sachivalayam).

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit your Local Secretariat
    Locate your designated Grama Sachivalayam (rural) or Ward Sachivalayam (urban). Working hours are generally 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM.
  2. Meet the Digital Assistant / VRO
    Request a New Rice Card Application form. You can also ask your assigned Grama/Ward Volunteer to help you procure and fill it out.
  3. Fill the Form & Attach Documents
    Fill in details like Head of Family, Aadhaar numbers of all members, address, and income details. Attach self-attested photocopies of Aadhaar, address proof, and income proof.
  4. Submit & E-KYC
    Submit the application to the Digital Assistant. They will enter the details online and may perform Aadhaar e-KYC (Biometric authentication) for the family members. You will receive an application number/receipt.
  5. Field Verification
    Your local Volunteer and the Village Revenue Officer (VRO) will conduct a physical verification at your home to confirm your eligibility and living conditions.
  6. Approval & Card Issuance
    Once verified and approved by the Tahsildar/MRO, the new Rice Card will be printed and handed over to you directly by your Volunteer or at the Secretariat.
Support: Your designated Grama/Ward Volunteer is your primary point of contact for tracking and assistance.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Your Application

Visit apseva.ap.gov.in
Enter your Application Number / Aadhaar Number to check status, or simply ask your local Volunteer.

Download Digital Rice Card

Once approved, you can download a copy of your Rice Card from the AP Seva portal or get a physical printout from your Secretariat.

Aadhaar e-KYC Completion

Ensure all family members complete their biometric authentication (e-KYC) at the nearest Fair Price Shop (FPS) or Secretariat to start receiving rations.

Raise a Grievance

If your application is delayed, call the AP Seva Helpline at 1902 or the PDS Helpline at 1967, or raise a ticket at your Sachivalayam.
